c74object.name: analyze~
c74object.digest: Performs spectral transience analysis on an audio signal
c74object.description:
The `analyze~` object performs real-time spectral analysis of an incoming audio signal. It utilizes an STFT-based Mel-spectrogram and Spectral Flux to detect transient peaks and calculate various transience-related metrics. The analysis is performed on a 1ms-hop basis and utilizes a background thread for expensive calculations to maintain audio stability.
This object uses a unified 15.2-second stateful analysis window, ensuring parity with offline Python-based analysis. Transients are detected across four frequency bands (Sub, Bass/Low-Mid, High-Mid, Treble) and assigned a "Resonance Score" based on their historical relationship with other peaks in a 5-second lookback buffer.

inlet.description: Accepts the audio signal to be analyzed.

outlet.description: Outputs a two-part list whenever a transient peak is detected. The first element is the frequency band index (0:Sub, 1:Bass, 2:Mid, 3:Hi) and the second is the calculated transience score (Flux * Resonance Qualifier sum).
Outputs the average rating score calculated over the analysis history.
Outputs the current standard deviation of the analysis scores.
Outputs a score representing the spectral contrast detected in the signal.
Outputs the highest peak deviation currently observed in the analyzed signal.
